test.command: bun test --max-concurrency=6 apps/dashboard/src apps/api/src packages/api/src packages/auth/src packages/db/src packages/env/src

typecheck.command: bun run check-types && bun x tsc -p apps/dashboard/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/api/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/auth/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/db/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/env/tsconfig.json --noEmit

lint.command: bun x ultracite check apps/dashboard/src apps/api/src packages/api/src packages/auth/src packages/db/src packages/env/src

blockingIssues.description: apps/dashboard/src/lib/return-to.ts accepts slash-backslash return_to values such as /\\evil.com because it only rejects // prefixes, leaving an open-redirect path that violates VAL-AUTH-002.

packages/api/src/onboarding.ts only enforces alphabetic fixed-length currency/country codes, so invalid codes like ZZZ and ZZ pass both schema and browser validation instead of being blocked client-side as required by VAL-ONBOARD-002.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented in .factory/library/architecture.md that team switching is membership-first: team.list exposes membershipId and user.switchTeam consumes membershipId.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten worker skill-compliance reporting so handoffs cannot claim followedProcedure=true when required skill invocations or required direct validation steps were skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The current-team review found api-parity-worker-required authenticated/team-ready API checks were skipped while the handoff still marked followedProcedure=true, and the protected-shell review found dashboard-parity-worker-required ultracite invocation was skipped with the same claim.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align feature-level verification contracts with .factory/services.yaml or clarify in skills which source is authoritative when feature-scoped validator commands are narrower than the shared services manifest.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for midday-request-context-and-current-team-primitives found its feature contract requires API-only validators, while .factory/services.yaml only exposes the broader cross-workspace commands, making the literal skill instruction to use services.yaml impossible to follow for that feature.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify which Midday onboarding references are relevant for Faworra so workers borrow routing and validation patterns without assuming Midday's larger multi-step onboarding flow should be mirrored.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The onboarding-bootstrap-recovery-parity review found the worker had to infer which Midday onboarding patterns mattered for Faworra's much smaller bootstrap form, and the handoff explicitly suggested this skill clarification.
